commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Sonnet 5 adaptive-thinking request normalization across Anthropic-family transports, simple-completion/session reasoning, provider helpers, SDK/type surfaces, and tests.

PR surface: Source +350, Tests +795, Generated 0. Total +1145 across 38 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main does not classify `claude-sonnet-5` as mandatory adaptive for Anthropic Messages, so Sonnet 5 can still reach legacy manual-thinking and configured-field payload paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor should repair the conflicted code and provide real after-fix provider proof; ClawSweeper should not queue repair automation while the external proof gate is insufficient.

target.title: fix: support Claude Sonnet 5 adaptive thinking requests
target.body:
Closes #98386
Related: #98254

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users selecting Claude Sonnet 5 could hit Anthropic API request failures or lose the requested thinking mode when using default, adaptive, low, or off thinking settings.

Claude Sonnet 5 uses Anthropic adaptive thinking semantics and rejects legacy non-default sampling fields on thinking requests. The existing request path could still send older enabled-thinking payloads, force unsupported tool-choice combinations, or normalize `adaptive` before the Sonnet 5 provider boundary could handle it.

## Why This Change Was Made

This updates the Anthropic-family request paths to use Sonnet 5 adaptive thinking correctly: default and explicit `adaptive` omit `output_config.effort`, explicit `low` and other effort levels map to `output_config.effort`, and explicit `off` disables thinking where the Sonnet 5 API allows it. Sonnet 5 requests also strip `temperature`, `top_p`, and `top_k` after payload hooks so migrated requests follow the provider API contract.

The scope intentionally keeps non-Anthropic providers limited to boundary normalization. `adaptive` is now a public simple/session reasoning option, so OpenAI and Mistral normalize it before their provider-specific clamps, while Google keeps its existing native adaptive behavior because its provider policy already advertises adaptive support. This PR does not add new OpenAI, Gemini, or Mistral thinking features.

## User Impact

Users can configure Claude Sonnet 5 with the existing OpenClaw thinking controls and get the expected behavior:

- no explicit setting: Sonnet 5 uses adaptive thinking by default without changing the OpenClaw global default policy for other models
- `adaptive`: sends native adaptive thinking without an effort override
- `low` and other supported levels: sends adaptive thinking with the requested Anthropic effort
- `off`: disables Sonnet 5 thinking instead of being silently forced back on

## Evidence

- `git diff --check`
- Secret-pattern scan over the HEAD diff returned no matches
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed, 19 tests
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed, 107 tests
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/provider-policy-api.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed, 8 tests
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed, 44 tests
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot` passed, 28 tests
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src-sonnet5-preserve.tsbuildinfo` passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-sonnet5-preserve.tsbuildinfo` passed
- Direct stale-catalog assertion confirmed Sonnet 5 Anthropic and Bedrock profiles still expose `adaptive` when catalog rows have `reasoning: false`
- Static review found no actionable correctness issues in the changed provider/session reasoning paths
- Redacted live smoke before/after: current release returned 400 for Sonnet 5 adaptive/low; patched runtime succeeded for default, adaptive, low, and off

Known local check gaps:

- `node scripts/build-all.mjs` could not complete locally because pnpm aborted a non-TTY `node_modules` purge prompt
- `node scripts/check.mjs` could not complete locally because the pnpm store/preflight step failed with `ERR_SQLITE_ERROR: unable to open database file`
- The review sandbox could not run the repo test wrapper because `.git/openclaw-local-checks/heavy-check.lock` is read-only there

AI-assisted contribution disclosure: this patch and PR text were prepared with AI assistance and verified with the focused tests and checks listed above.
target.author: sunjae-k
